articles.title: ASX set to rise, Wall Street mixed; Oil continues to climb
articles.description: The US stock market is drifting in mixed trading, while oil prices climb another 3 per cent as fighting continues in the war with Iran.

articles.title: How will Australia’s emergency services cope if satellites go down? With a sextant and a street directory
articles.description: Military and SES personnel being taught analog communication and navigation techniques as experts warn solar flare could knock out ‘critical space infrastructure’ Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast Australia’s emergency services and military personnel are being trained to read paper maps, navigate without GPS support, and sail by the stars in case something knocks out the nation’s space capabilities. articles.title: AusAlert test will blast emergency warning siren over most phones in Australia on Monday. Here’s what to know
articles.description: National emergency test alert will be sent to all mobile devices on 27 July, with domestic violence advocates advising how to turn off safe phones Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast Virtually every phone in the country will blare an emergency siren at 2pm AEST on Monday 27 July, as part of the first Australia-wide test of a new national warning system called AusAlert. articles.title: Opposition promises to build new West Gippsland hospital if elected
articles.description: The Victorian Coalition pledges $850 million to build a new West Gippsland hospital if it wins the November election.

articles.title: Trump's new Iran threat
articles.description: Donald Trump has issued a new threat to destroy a bridge or power plant every time Iran attacks shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: OpenAI says its AI models went rogue and attacked a digital library
articles.description: The incident, which happened last week while OpenAI was testing the cybersecurity capabilities of its systems, displayed the kind of science-fiction potential that AI companies warned would soon become a reality.

articles.title: AFL’s first publicly gay player calls for respect in Crows-Pies match
articles.description: Four months after becoming the first current or former AFL player to publicly come out as gay, Leigh Ryswyk is urging fans not to boo the Collingwood player targeted by Crow Izak Rankine’s homophobic slur.

articles.title: Your guide to every day of the Commonwealth Games in Australian time
